exercises.prompt: Which 5 cards have the longest names? Return name and its character count as name_length, longest first, breaking ties alphabetically by name.
exercises.referenceSql: SELECT name, LENGTH(name) AS name_length FROM cards ORDER BY LENGTH(name) DESC, name LIMIT 5
hints: LENGTH(name) gives each name's character count, and you can sort by that same expression.
ORDER BY LENGTH(name) DESC, name, then LIMIT 5.

```sql
SELECT name, LENGTH(name) AS name_length FROM cards ORDER BY LENGTH(name) DESC, name LIMIT 5
```

exercises.prompt: Which starting letters are most common among card names? Take the first character of each name and count how many cards begin with it. Return that character as first_letter and the count as card_count for the 5 most common, most common first, breaking ties alphabetically by first_letter.
exercises.referenceSql: SELECT SUBSTR(name, 1, 1) AS first_letter, COUNT(*) AS card_count FROM cards GROUP BY first_letter ORDER BY card_count DESC, first_letter LIMIT 5
hints: SUBSTR(name, 1, 1) extracts the first character; group by it to count names per starting letter.
GROUP BY SUBSTR(name, 1, 1), ORDER BY card_count DESC, first_letter, LIMIT 5.

```sql
SELECT SUBSTR(name, 1, 1) AS first_letter, COUNT(*) AS card_count FROM cards GROUP BY first_letter ORDER BY card_count DESC, first_letter LIMIT 5
```

exercises.prompt: Build a display label for each DIVINE-attribute card by joining its name, a space, and its attribute in parentheses — for example 'Obelisk the Tormentor (DIVINE)'. Return only that single column aliased as label, ordered by name ascending.
exercises.referenceSql: SELECT name || ' (' || attribute || ')' AS label FROM cards WHERE attribute = 'DIVINE' ORDER BY name
hints: The || operator concatenates strings; you can chain several together with literal text in between.
name || ' (' || attribute || ')' AS label, filtered WHERE attribute = 'DIVINE', ORDER BY name.

```sql
SELECT name || ' (' || attribute || ')' AS label FROM cards WHERE attribute = 'DIVINE' ORDER BY name
```

exercises.prompt: The archive wants Blue-Eyes card names shown with spaces instead of hyphens. For every card whose name starts with 'Blue-Eyes', return the original name and a version with every hyphen replaced by a single space, aliased as spaced_name. Order by name ascending.
exercises.referenceSql: SELECT name, REPLACE(name, '-', ' ') AS spaced_name FROM cards WHERE name LIKE 'Blue-Eyes%' ORDER BY name
hints: REPLACE(text, from, to) swaps every occurrence of one substring for another.
REPLACE(name, '-', ' ') AS spaced_name, filtered WHERE name LIKE 'Blue-Eyes%', ORDER BY name.

```sql
SELECT name, REPLACE(name, '-', ' ') AS spaced_name FROM cards WHERE name LIKE 'Blue-Eyes%' ORDER BY name
```

exercises.prompt: The tournament announcer shouts every 'Dark Magician' archetype card name in all capitals. Return each card's name and its uppercased form as shout, ordered by name ascending.
exercises.referenceSql: SELECT name, UPPER(name) AS shout FROM cards WHERE archetype = 'Dark Magician' ORDER BY name
hints: UPPER(name) returns the name with every letter capitalized.
Select name and UPPER(name) AS shout, filtered WHERE archetype = 'Dark Magician', ORDER BY name.

```sql
SELECT name, UPPER(name) AS shout FROM cards WHERE archetype = 'Dark Magician' ORDER BY name
```

exercises.prompt: Bucket every card by the length of its name: 'Short' when the name is 15 characters or fewer, 'Medium' when it is 16 to 30 characters, and 'Long' when it is 31 or more. Return the bucket as length_bucket and the count as card_count, most numerous first, breaking ties alphabetically by length_bucket.
exercises.referenceSql: SELECT CASE WHEN LENGTH(name) <= 15 THEN 'Short' WHEN LENGTH(name) <= 30 THEN 'Medium' ELSE 'Long' END AS length_bucket, COUNT(*) AS card_count FROM cards GROUP BY length_bucket ORDER BY card_count DESC, length_bucket
hints: Feed LENGTH(name) into a CASE expression to turn a character count into a labeled bucket, then group by that label.
CASE WHEN LENGTH(name) <= 15 THEN 'Short' WHEN LENGTH(name) <= 30 THEN 'Medium' ELSE 'Long' END, then GROUP BY the bucket.

```sql
SELECT CASE WHEN LENGTH(name) <= 15 THEN 'Short' WHEN LENGTH(name) <= 30 THEN 'Medium' ELSE 'Long' END AS length_bucket, COUNT(*) AS card_count FROM cards GROUP BY length_bucket ORDER BY card_count DESC, length_bucket
```
